_README: AUTHORED columns for ENGINE_MAP.md. Never generated, never overwritten by scripts/engine_map.mjs.
purpose — one sentence. If a module needs two, it is probably two modules.
playerVisibleSurface — the control, receipt, panel or prose the PLAYER actually meets. Write NONE
when nothing reaches them. NONE is not a failure; some machinery is correctly invisible. It is a
FLAG: a module with NONE, no content dependency and no GM verb is the exact shape that produced
the eight built-and-unreached capabilities, and it is a different question from reachability —
skill_battle was reachable in principle and invisible in practice for months.
Backfill is incremental (ROUND 2 §5): a module gets its two lines when a spec touches it. The CI
gate WARNS on an undescribed module and FAILS on a half-described one, because a purpose with no
surface reads as complete when it is not.

namematch.js.purpose: The one name-resolution primitive — slugify and fuzzy-match an entity the same way everywhere, so codex, quests and inventory can never disagree about what the GM just named.
namematch.js.playerVisibleSurface: Indirect but constant: every id the player sees, and the quest panel's "couldn't match" note when the GM names something that does not resolve.

quests.js.purpose: Quests as typed state the GM drives through clamped ops — the model proposes, the engine adjudicates and refuses in the open.
quests.js.playerVisibleSurface: The quest panel: active quests, stage progress lines (✦ …), the resolution choice when every stage is done, and refusal notes when the GM names the wrong stage.

playerprofile.js.purpose: Player identity across characters, and the earned play-style (tendencies, aptitudes) that accrues on each character from how it is actually played.
playerprofile.js.playerVisibleSurface: The profile screen's play-style readout, and the character-select roster.

art.js.purpose: Images for characters, locations, items, NPCs and big moments, from either a static file or a generated prompt, behind one seam.
art.js.playerVisibleSurface: Every image in the app: location art, portraits, item art, the moment gallery.

npcs.js.purpose: NPC permanence — everyone met, authored or invented mid-scene, gets a durable registry entry the GM writes to through clamped npcUpdates.
npcs.js.playerVisibleSurface: The people list and each NPC's detail card: who they are, where you met, what has passed between you.

traditions.js.purpose: The Great Circle geometry — the 24-station ring, neighbours, antipodes and distances — read from content, never hardcoded.
traditions.js.playerVisibleSurface: The skill wheel's ring layout and the tradition labels shown on abilities and standing.

skilltree.js.purpose: Skill legibility and gating: tiers, attribute gates, and what is learnable now versus later.
skilltree.js.playerVisibleSurface: The skill wheel and skill graph — tier rings, locked/unlocked state, and the reason a locked ability is locked.

company.js.purpose: The unified company: companion, trainer, liaison, partner and ally are stacking ROLES a person in your party holds, each wired to the system that already implements it.
company.js.playerVisibleSurface: The company panel — who travels with you and what each one is to you.

reputation.js.purpose: Deeds are the source of truth and reputation is a VIEW over them — a community's opinion is the sum of the deeds it knows about.
reputation.js.playerVisibleSurface: The standing list on the character screen (band per people). Empty for most players today — the gap BATCH-12 §3 exists to close.

progression.js.purpose: Character growth with numbers you can point at — sub-attributes, levelling, ability acquisition and the receipts for each.
progression.js.playerVisibleSurface: Level-up prompts, the attribute allocation screen, new-ability offers, and the ✦ receipt lines when something is earned.

state.js.purpose: Content-pack loading and save/load — localStorage is authoritative, GitHub sync is optional on top.
state.js.playerVisibleSurface: Boot ("Loading the Valley"), the save/load controls, and the recovery-slot list when a save fails to write.

generate.js.purpose: The ONE generative path — authors a new NPC, location or arc in-grain for its locale, validates against the derived schema, resolves before minting so it never duplicates, and persists it.
generate.js.playerVisibleSurface: Any place or person that did not exist until the fiction needed it; the player meets these as ordinary world content.

places.js.purpose: Location permanence — durable changes, things left behind and discoveries that OUTLIVE the scene, fed back on every return visit.
places.js.playerVisibleSurface: The location panel's "what happened here" lines on returning somewhere you have been.

claude.js.purpose: Anthropic API transport, with MODEL_MAP as the single source of truth for task→model routing and a token/stop_reason log per call.
claude.js.playerVisibleSurface: NONE directly — but every latency the player feels and every API error card comes from here.

worldmap.js.purpose: The data-driven map foundation: stable auto-positioning for every location including ones minted mid-play, plus the three zoom tiers.
worldmap.js.playerVisibleSurface: The map screen and its World / Region / Location tier bar.

intent.js.purpose: Intent confirmation for costly acts — nothing irreversible or world-scale commits until the player confirms, the same shape character creation already uses.
intent.js.playerVisibleSurface: The confirmation card before a harmful or departing act, naming the cost in plain language.

waygate.js.purpose: The waygate network and its hub at the Crossing, including routing a GM's moveTo to a real gate rather than letting it mint a destination.
waygate.js.playerVisibleSurface: The waygate travel control and the destination list when standing at a gate.

gm.js.purpose: Assembles the GM prompt and parses the structured reply — code owns the rules and the dice, the model owns the words.
gm.js.playerVisibleSurface: All narration and the choice buttons; also the salvage path that keeps prose on screen when the reply parses badly.

substrate.js.purpose: The SECOND difficulty map — the nanite lattice varies in density by place and each tradition is tuned to a band of it, so where you stand changes what you can do.
substrate.js.playerVisibleSurface: The success-chance readout before acting (the penalty is folded in, per SNG-116) and the thin/crowded lattice note the GM works into prose. No named receipt of its own.

practice.js.purpose: Competency as the residue of attention — an engine-owned ledger of ability use and co-activation that unlocks ranks at zero skill-point cost.
practice.js.playerVisibleSurface: Aspiration progress and the unlock notices when practice crosses a threshold.

party.js.purpose: Shared-world play — scene publication, the open-scene index, and merge-safe writes so two players in one scene cannot clobber each other.
party.js.playerVisibleSurface: The party beat feed and the presence indicator when someone else is in your scene.

gm_registry.js.purpose: The declared table of everything the GM can be told — one row per context block, iterated at every call site so a capability cannot be built and left unreachable.
gm_registry.js.playerVisibleSurface: NONE. It is the §23 wiring contract itself; the player meets it only as a GM that knows things.
